mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns as capital flows into risk assets.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance against a zero-mean null hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity sell-offs due to recession fears.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4pp in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.4pp monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a positive rank IC over 60-day forward returns across multiple expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation, a contraction in the industrial production index signals supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins and force negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during high CPIAUCSL periods yields positive 20-day forward returns in over 60% of observations, breaking the margin compression channel.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above the long-run trend rate confirms economic expansion that supports corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above trend fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a rank IC greater than 0.01 over a 5-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, the cost of capital becomes prohibitive for highly leveraged firms, triggering a sustained drawdown in equity valuations over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets record positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of periods where the federal funds rate remains above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Concurrent PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to generate a positive rank IC for 20-day forward returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery phase, a sharp rebound in the industrial production index signals restored manufacturing capacity that eliminates supply chain premiums and lifts equity prices.
failure_condition: INDPRO rebounds during recovery phases fail to predict positive 5-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy dropping below 52 percent.
